document.name: Staten Island Community Partnership — North Shore Families
document.description:
The Staten Island Community Partnership (SICP) is a family-centered community program supported by The New York Foundling and funded by the NYC Administration for Children’s Services. Its official site describes impactful, educational, and skills-building encounters that enrich the lives of children and families on Staten Island’s North Shore, working with residents and local partners to build a safety net of support.

Programs and services pages note weekly SICP Free Market pantry shopping for North Shore families, monthly membership meetings (including virtual SICP Monthly Meetings listed on the events calendar), community ambassador outreach, and family events such as PB&J (Pajamas, Books, and Joining Families Together). The partnership connects residents to healthcare, mental health, counseling, and other resources through its network.

Published contact: 1 Edgewater Street, Suite 350, Staten Island, NY 10305; phone (917) 485-7608.

Sources: https://sicommunitypartnership.org/ https://sicommunitypartnership.org/about-us/ https://sicommunitypartnership.org/programs-and-services/ https://sicommunitypartnership.org/contact-us/
